name: Cote du Chateau de Montjuic Climb
roadSegment: Cote du Chateau de Montjuic / Montjuic Castle climb sector
estimatedPassageTime: 16:52 / 17:07 / 17:23 expected; slow estimates to 17:42 on final passage
summary: Best race-value Montjuic option for Stage 2 because the official route climbs the Chateau de Montjuic three times.
whyItMatters: The climb is listed three times at 1.6 km and 9.3%, so riders should slow substantially and the group can see multiple passages if positioned legally.

access.estimated_route_from_base: Approach via Montjuic on foot before final-circuit controls; exact route from base depends on lodging and closures.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: Normal access can involve funicular/cable car/bus approaches to Montjuic, but race-day operation and crowd controls are unknown.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: Walking in early is the safest planning assumption. Cycling access after closures must be verified.
access.road_closure_notes: Unknown; no 2026 Barcelona closure order found for the climb.

pros: Three official race passages on the same climb.
Steep gradient should make riders easier to see and identify.
Iconic Barcelona/Montjuic setting with strong Tour atmosphere.
cons: Likely one of the most crowded public areas on the Barcelona stages.
Castle-area roads and narrow bends may have restricted or unsafe standing areas.
Bathroom, food, and shade are not guaranteed once the final circuit is sealed.

logistics.parking: Unknown; do not plan on driving to the castle area on race day.
logistics.transit: Normal Montjuic options exist, but event-day service must be checked with TMB/Barcelona.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs official Barcelona Stage 2 circuit closure map.

crowdRisk: high: repeated steep climb, castle landmark, and final circuit make this a marquee spectator location.
bathroomFoodProximity: Unknown on race day. Castle/park facilities and nearby kiosks may not remain accessible after barriers.
escapeRoute: After the convoy clears, walk away from the summit/castle bottleneck toward Miramar, Poble-sec, or Placa d'Espanya depending on police directions.
safetyAssessment: Avoid blind bends, narrow shoulders, and any location where fans would spill into the climbing lane; use outside-barrier standing space only.

timing.best_arrival_window: Morning to early afternoon; be in place before final-circuit closure and caravan passage.
timing.race_day_risk: High due to access, crowding, and climb-road control uncertainty.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 2 official page
sources.note: Supports Stage 2 timing, final circuit, three Cote du Chateau de Montjuic passages, climb length, elevation, and gradient.

sources.title: TDF trip data official route note
sources.note: Official ASO ArcGIS layer referenced by the app for Stage 2 route geometry; coordinate matches the app's selected Montjuic Castle point.

sources.title: Montjuic background
sources.note: Supports Montjuic hill/castle context and normal access background; not a race-day logistics source.

open_questions: Which parts of the Chateau de Montjuic climb will be public versus restricted?
Will spectators be allowed to remain between repeated circuit passages, and on which side?
Where are confirmed toilets, water, food, and first-aid points near the climb?
